meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve no version control (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Project Template to Solve Version Control
content.seo_description: Stop losing your code. Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to master version control as a beginner. Start building today.
content.answer_block: Stop manually managing file backups. By implementing the Antigravity project template, you establish an automated, structured environment that integrates version control from day one. Combined with Claude Code, this workflow allows you to ship production-ready features while ensuring your project history remains safe, searchable, and professional for future employers.
content.intro:
You’ve spent hours crafting the perfect function, only to accidentally overwrite it or lose your progress in a maze of 'final_v2_real.js' files. It is the silent killer of student projects: the lack of a formal version control strategy. You feel the anxiety of every save, terrified that a single wrong move will erase your hard work.

But professional engineers don't rely on luck. They rely on architecture. By adopting the Antigravity project template, you move from 'coding in the dark' to building with the precision of a senior dev. This isn't just about saving files; it's about building a robust foundation that turns your school project into a portfolio-ready asset.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Start by creating a clean, versioned environment. This prevents the 'messy directory' problem and ensures every file has a home.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the standard Antigravity starter template with TypeScript and automatic Git initialization.

steps.title: Configure Atomic Commits
steps.description: Learn to commit your changes in small, logical chunks. This is how you track progress and revert specific bugs without losing the entire project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a git pre-commit hook that validates my code style before allowing a commit.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure husky and lint-staged for this project'

steps.title: Automate Documentation with Claude
steps.description: Never write manual READMEs again. Use Claude Code to analyze your code and generate documentation that stays in sync with your version history.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current codebase and generate a comprehensive README.md that explains how to run the project and the current feature set.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a professional README.md based on the current file structure'

steps.title: Implement Feature Branching
steps.description: Master the art of isolating features. By creating a branch for every new task, you protect your main code from accidental breakage.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a new git branch named 'feature/authentication' and guide me through the implementation steps.

steps.title: Deploy to Version-Controlled Hosting
steps.description: Connect your repository to a cloud provider so that every 'git push' triggers a live update of your site.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide the steps to connect this local repo to GitHub and configure automated deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'provide instructions for setting up GitHub Actions for automated deployment'

pro_tips: Commit messages should follow the Conventional Commits specification to make your history readable by recruiters.
Use a .gitignore file immediately to keep your node_modules and API keys out of your public history.
Never merge directly into main; always use Pull Requests to review your own code before finalizing.
Treat your commit history as a journal of your learning journey; it’s more impressive to see iterative progress than one giant 'finished' commit.
Automate your environment variables using a .env.example file so you never accidentally commit secrets to GitHub.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully pushed your first version-controlled project to GitHub, start contributing to one open-source project to see how professional teams handle large-scale version control.
content.conversion_block: You now have the tools to ensure your code is never lost and your history is always professional. Stop struggling with manual backups and start building like a pro. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
